findings.message: New data-access path get_item_by_name filters on Item.name, which has no index (see models.py / migration 0001_initial — only id is a primary key, name is a plain String(255) column). This equality lookup is a sequential scan, and unlike the rest of the request path it carries no per-query latency or row-count metric: the HTTP middleware only records whole-request latency and SQLAlchemy auto-instrumentation only emits a generic span, so a slow scan on a growing items table is invisible to the metrics layer. name is also not unique, so .first() silently returns one of N matches with no observability into how many rows the predicate actually touched.
findings.suggestion: Add an index on items.name (e.g. index=True on the mapped_column plus an Alembic migration with op.create_index), and surface the query's latency / matched-row count to the metrics registry (or at minimum log it with the correlation id) so the scan cost on this unindexed lookup is observable.
